description.p: Copperline is a cycle-driven emulator for the Commodore Amiga (OCS, ECS and AGA chipsets). It models the underlying hardware -- the 68000 CPU, the Agnus, Denise and Paula custom chips, the CIAs, the floppy controller and the chip-bus timing -- rather than individual titles, so demos and games that depend on precise timing behave as they did on real machines.

ul.li: OCS, ECS and AGA display paths, including HAM, HAM8 and AGA FMODE
Cycle-accurate Copper, blitter and chip-bus arbitration
A500, A600, A1200, A4000, CDTV and CD32 machine profiles
Gayle and A2091 hard-disk support, plus directory-as-hard-disk
Save states and deterministic input recording and replay
Ships with the open-source AROS Kickstart replacement so it boots out of the box without a copyrighted ROM

description.p: A real Kickstart ROM and disk or hard-disk images are required to run commercial Amiga software; these are not included.

screenshot.caption: Spaceballs State of the Art running in Copperline
